label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what you should do when communicating with a pen pal. The given text has some advice on this. Let me read through the relevant part again. The passage says: "Make sure to share all letters and e-mails with your parents so they know you are safe. If something seems _ , stop writing letters. Never meet a pen pal in person unless a grown-up is with you." So the key points here are about safety. Sharing your communications with parents, not meeting without a grown-up, and stopping if something feels off. Looking at the options: A) Keep all the pen pal's letters and e-mails in private. But the text says to share them with parents, so this is the opposite of what's advised. So A is probably wrong. B) Meet him/her with one of your classmates. The text says not to meet unless a grown-up is there. So meeting with a classmate (another kid) is not safe according to the passage. So B is incorrect. C) Be sure you are safe at any time. This is a general statement that aligns with the advice given. Since the text emphasizes safety by sharing with parents and not meeting without an adult, option C is likely
